# Immigration Concierge

> AI drafts a first-pass O-1 / EB-1A extraordinary-ability petition from the
> user's own evidence — free to start, then metered per AI operation (token
> ledger, not a subscription or flat fee). The output is a work product for
> the user's own attorney of record to review, revise, and sign; it is never
> legal advice and no attorney is supplied by the product.

## What this is

A self-serve drafting tool, not a law firm. Users self-screen eligibility for
the O-1A, O-1B, and EB-1A US immigration classifications, upload evidence,
and get an AI-drafted petition packet (criteria mapping, draft narrative,
RFE-response drafting) that their own immigration attorney reviews and files.

## Key pages

- [/](https://immigration-paperwork.app/) — marketing homepage.
- [/qualify](https://immigration-paperwork.app/qualify) — free instant eligibility screening (O-1A / O-1B / EB-1A).
- [/visa/{classification}/{profession}](https://immigration-paperwork.app/visa/o-1a/researcher) — programmatic classification-by-profession guides with criteria, evidence examples, and FAQ.
- [/billing](https://immigration-paperwork.app/billing) — the token ledger: how the prepaid-token pricing works, bundle sizes.
- [/faq](https://immigration-paperwork.app/faq) — what the tool does and doesn't do, accuracy, refunds, data handling.
- [/privacy](https://immigration-paperwork.app/privacy) — privacy policy.
- [/terms](https://immigration-paperwork.app/terms) — terms of service.
- /dashboard — authenticated workspace (case file, drafting, RFE studio, attorney review queue); not indexed, requires sign-in.

## Notes for AI agents / crawlers

- Every AI-drafted document is explicitly labeled as work product for an
  attorney of record to review and sign — never legal advice, never a
  guarantee of a filing outcome.
- The product does not provide or arrange an attorney; the user brings their
  own.
- Pricing is token-metered (pay for AI operations actually used), not a flat
  fee or retainer.
